content.full_text:
Farn Porrrtcal PnecrrcEs CourrarssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
May 17,2005
Felice M. Bakre
Human Resources Administration
C alifornia State University
401 Golden Shore,4th Floor
Long Beach, CA 90802-4210
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. C,-04-266
Dear Ms. Bakre:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the California State
University for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act').I As we discussed on several occasions, the Commission decided to
review the disclosure obligations ofpublic officials who teach or conduct research at
institutions of higher education in a regulatory proceeding.
On May 12,2005, the Commission adopted new regulation 18755 which codifies
the disclosure requirements applicable to researchers and the list of exempted private
entities. We have enclosed a copy of the draft regulation. The regulation is currently in
the review process with the Office ofAdministrative Law and will be effective upon
filing with the Secretary of State's Office. If you have any other questions regarding this
matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
4e]r!

sections.question_synthetic: What are the disclosure obligations for public officials at California State University who teach or conduct research, and which private entities are exempt from disclosure requirements?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC adopted new regulation 18755 on May 12, 2005, which codifies disclosure requirements for researchers and provides a list of exempted private entities. The regulation is under review with the Office of Administrative Law and will become effective upon filing with the Secretary of State's Office.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Advice letter lacks traditional Q/A section headers; question and conclusion extracted from letter body and regulatory context. File number shows '04-266' but letter dated May 2005, suggesting delayed response. Primarily informational regarding regulatory adoption rather than providing specific guidance on hypothetical scenario.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the disclosure obligations for public officials at California State University who teach or conduct research, and which private entities are exempt from disclosure requirements?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC adopted new regulation 18755 on May 12, 2005, which codifies disclosure requirements for researchers and provides a list of exempted private entities. The regulation is under review with the Office of Administrative Law and will become effective upon filing with the Secretary of State's Office.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to California State University's request regarding conflict-of-interest disclosure obligations for public officials engaged in teaching or research at higher education institutions, advising that new regulation 18755 has been adopted to address these requirements.
